item.title: Re: Spine
item.content:encoded:
The skip is also effected by fiction and dreams. So I just image that all of the logs are made up by the foundation.
So it is fiction within fiction fictionception.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sounds like a job for me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ooooh, I see… clever. I really like it.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This means that some poor dude at some Site has a job to basically write one over-the-top snuff story a day in order to keep this … thing… happy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This took me a while to wrap my head around this one, but once I figured out what was going on with the test logs I completely fell in love with this article. Well played indeed. +1

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded: Did they lose? I assumed since it could move into new metaphysical locations, the foundation essentially "trapped" it in the fictional testing logs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At first, I thought this was just another SCP which had destroyed its containment site. And then I was like… oh. And very satisfied to have gotten it correctly when looking at the comments. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh my goodness. I've read a lot of gory articles before, but none have made me scratch my head and then slap myself in the face as hard as this one. It's glorious and the subtext present still leaves me with chills.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: wow, that… actually makes this SCP make sense. and flips this from a no-vote to a +1 for me. that was well done, and very tricksy of you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"SCP-4296 appears to prefer physical injury and torture to mental or emotional distress - although it will still respond to it - but will not respond to any human suffering that concludes with the death of the human in question."
and
"SCP-4296 has proven capable of physical, metaphysical and pataphysical traversal, appearing in physical locations, dreams and even narrative works such as films and television shows on these occasions."

Tricky, tricky, tricky.
+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Best take on the "compulsion to harm" cliche I've seen in ages. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The blackboxing should be used sparingly. While I understand the purpose it serves, it just doesn't really look good when I read it and it just reminds me of a newbie's coldpost.

Not that I think it makes the entire article terrible in that regard, it's just not to my taste when it comes to the presentation. That's all.

item.title: Site Criticism Team - Review
item.content:encoded:
+[Critique] Click to Access
-[Click to Hide Text]

What the fuck?

I'll admit that I didn't know what to take away from this at first. Seemed like something that they could probably just contain with breaking a D-Class's fingers once a day and call it. So imagine my face when I read "non-lethally dismembers and beheads".

I really like this piece. The juxtaposition between the actions of the personnel, the reaction of the SCP, and the overall premise of "this is to keep this thing secret" is just wild and I love it. I also appreciate that, while this is certainly a contender for the End of Death canon, that it doesn't really fit into it when everything is considered. This is its own separate universe, and it's clear from the interview log.

I do have my own thoughts as to what this piece could symbolize, but I think that would be projecting onto something that is just as satisfying being "what you see is what you get". This is an upvote from me.

EDIT: I just read your spoiler up top. I really made this into something it isn't. Whoops!


Disclaimer: Opinions expressed in this review do not reflect the opinions of SCP Wiki staff as a whole. Please respond to RockTeethMothEyes or contact the captain of the Site Criticism Team, SoullessSingularity, for any questions or concerns relating to this review.
*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I can understand that. I think it's pretty important to the ending, though, so it's not something I'm gonna change. Sorry it bothered you here, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay. I feel a *little* better now. I have trouble with spotting unreliable narrators and assumed that everything was actually happening.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not a big fan of the constant blackboxing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Spoilers
Close

You've pretty much got it, yeah! Although the test logs aren't the only part that's fictional…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What. What the fuck?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I want to understand just one thing.
Does this mean that the SCP was contained in fake test logs?
Like they keep torturing fake characters for the
sake of containment?

item.title: +1... but dear god
item.content:encoded:
Glad I could help out with this one, this is a delightfully disturbing skip! Though I’m gonna have nightmares of the test logs and shudder mc moany guy 4296.

Anyway, +1
From IRC chat to the main list, I would say that enjoyed reading this. I forgot to say on the chat that the tests give this article an emotional impact to the reader and that's why I liked it.

item.title: Spine
item.content:encoded:
How the fuck was it possible for him to hang himself with his own spine. Jesus Christ.

+1

I'm always a fan of scips that can quickly and concisely deliver a very specific emotion. There are plenty of examples of subtle writing being used to great emotional effect on this wiki, but this is some of the most effective blunt force emotion I've read in a while. Every single section of this had some part that made me do a double take, and it left me feeling disturbed, disgusted, and in some way sad for the foundation we all know and (to some extent) love essentially losing to this anomaly. Overall, well done.
(shudders and moans)

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded: Thanks to Cregan_Stark, Mew-ltiverse, Henzoid, Alex13902 and Gamesofmax for their valued feedback!
